# Questions in Livestream 193

Live Stream #193: Back in the Saddle.

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/F3BEjnAqgWw?t=624" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">10:24</a> | What should we make of the reported altar discovery near Jerusalem that has been linked to David and the First Temple period? | He said it may be a real First Temple period ritual site, but not a Davidic one, since the evidence sounded more 8th century BC than David's time. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/F3BEjnAqgWw?t=750" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">12:30</a> | How long does it take for a destroyed city to become buried underground? | He said Near Eastern tells can begin forming within a few centuries, though cities are usually being buried into a mound rather than simply sinking beneath the ground. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/F3BEjnAqgWw?t=879" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">14:39</a> | Is there direct or indirect evidence that Joseph could have been vizier in the Hyksos period? | He said yes, arguing that details such as Joseph's use of a chariot fit a Hyksos-period setting better than many later models. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/F3BEjnAqgWw?t=1159" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">19:19</a> | Is it significant that the parallel to 1 Kings 6:1 in 2 Chronicles 3:2 does not include the 480 years? | He said not really, warning against building an argument from absence and noting the two texts are doing different things. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/F3BEjnAqgWw?t=1439" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">23:59</a> | What will be the greatest theological or philosophical issue for the next generation of Christians? | He said soteriology will be central, especially how changing moral assumptions relate to sin, grace, and God's plan of salvation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/F3BEjnAqgWw?t=1805" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">30:05</a> | How can the church overcome emotionalism? | He said expository preaching is the main antidote, because churches built only on emotional stimulation produce shallow roots. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/F3BEjnAqgWw?t=2359" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">39:19</a> | What is case law, and how can someone be shown that Old Testament law often works that way? | He said Old Testament legal material frequently gives case examples with maximum penalties rather than exhaustive statutory code. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/F3BEjnAqgWw?t=2647" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">44:07</a> | Who is the second "he" in Exodus 34:28? | He said the antecedent chain points to Moses writing the second set of tablets, while the first set was written by the finger of God. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/F3BEjnAqgWw?t=2799" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">46:39</a> | Why do the NIV and NLT translate Milcom as Molech in 1 Kings 11:5? | He traced it to a traditional rendering, likely influenced by older confusion between Milcom and Molech rather than the Hebrew text itself. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/F3BEjnAqgWw?t=3180" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">53:00</a> | Is Astarte the same as Asherah? | No. He distinguished Astarte from Asherah and said the relationship of Asherah traditions across the Levant is more complicated. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/F3BEjnAqgWw?t=3298" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">54:58</a> | In Exodus 40:18, why did Moses erect the tabernacle? | He said it was a first-time exception before the tabernacle and priests were consecrated; afterward, that work belonged to the designated Levitical servants. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/F3BEjnAqgWw?t=3467" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">57:47</a> | Was Moses considered a priest? | No. He said Moses was considered a prophet, while Aaron and Aaron's line were designated for priestly service. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/F3BEjnAqgWw?t=3524" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">58:44</a> | How should we interpret Jeremiah being longer in the Masoretic Text than in the Septuagint, and what about the Qumran manuscripts? | He said he would need to examine the evidence more closely and noted that Qumran may help, but he had not studied that issue enough to decide. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/F3BEjnAqgWw?t=3713" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:01:53</a> | What does "soothing aroma" mean regarding sacrifices? | He said it refers to God being pleased to have relationship with his people through properly offered sacrifices that cover sin in the old system. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/F3BEjnAqgWw?t=3952" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:05:52</a> | Does Moses parallel himself as Horus and Pharaoh as Set at the beginning of Exodus? | He answered no, saying the Exodus narrative is doing something else with the Moses-Pharaoh contrast. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/F3BEjnAqgWw?t=4310" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:11:50</a> | Could Zipporah have been Midianite by nationality and Kushite by ethnic heritage? | He said no, arguing that Kushites and Midianites should not be conflated and that attempts to connect Zipporah to the Cushite wife in Numbers 12 are weak. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/F3BEjnAqgWw?t=4506" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:15:06</a> | What was the job of the Medjay; were they like Egyptian sheriffs? | He described the Medjay as peace officers, guards, constables, and community leaders trusted by Pharaoh rather than as assassins or modern sheriffs. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/F3BEjnAqgWw?t=4992" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:23:12</a> | Was ancient racism more about descent than appearance? | He said that is probably right: Egyptian xenophobia was real, but it was more tied to origin, identity, and group descent than modern racial categories. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/F3BEjnAqgWw?t=5232" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:27:12</a> | How long would Moses have been weaned by his mother? | He said the exact answer is unknown, but Egyptian weaning could plausibly have taken several years, perhaps around three to five. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/F3BEjnAqgWw?t=5522" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:32:02</a> | In Leviticus 2:11-12, what is wrong with leaven and honey? | He said leaven was associated with mold and decay, while raw honey could contain insect parts and was not ritually appropriate for offerings. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/F3BEjnAqgWw?t=5900" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:38:20</a> | What is the salt of the covenant in Leviticus 2:13? | He said the literal salt in offerings symbolizes the covenant's preserving and purifying function, not that the covenant itself is literal salt. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/F3BEjnAqgWw?t=6355" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:45:55</a> | Were sin and guilt offerings common in the ancient Near East, and what was their purpose? | He said offerings were common, but Israel's sin and guilt offerings differed from feeding-the-gods systems because they dealt with covering sin, guilt, purification, vows, and thanksgiving. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/F3BEjnAqgWw?t=6539" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:48:59</a> | What does it mean to say the Levites are the true Israelites? | He said the phrase depends on who is using it, can become loaded language, and is not a good factual way to describe Israelite identity. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/F3BEjnAqgWw?t=6643" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:50:43</a> | What crisis occurred in Egypt during Joseph's lifetime? | He pointed to the famine in Genesis, with Joseph managing storage and distribution through that crisis. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/F3BEjnAqgWw?t=6836" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:53:56</a> | Who is Ishtar, and when was she worshiped? | He identified Ishtar as the Assyrian name for Inanna and connected Astarte with the Levantine form of that broader goddess tradition. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/F3BEjnAqgWw?t=7058" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:57:38</a> | How does ethnic geography relate to Anatolia, "east," and the Hittites? | He said Greek and Israelite ethnic geographies use different reference points, so Anatolia as "east" reflects Greek geography rather than Israelite usage. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/F3BEjnAqgWw?t=7224" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:00:24</a> | Were the Hittites around during the time of Jesus? | He said not as a political kingdom, though remnants of Hittite religion may have survived into the early Christian centuries. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/F3BEjnAqgWw?t=7372" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:02:52</a> | Who was your best beta reader, and why was it Mama? | He said each beta reader brought something different, with Mama especially good at detecting subtext and others helping with plot threads and other issues. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/F3BEjnAqgWw?t=8398" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:19:58</a> | Why do people trash Leviticus when it is rewarding to read? | He said many readers react against Leviticus because they misunderstand its genre and legal setting rather than reading it on its own terms. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/F3BEjnAqgWw?t=8537" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:22:17</a> | How did Egyptians view the religions of other nations? | He said Egyptians often incorporated foreign deities into their own pantheon, including gods from Nubia and the Levant. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/F3BEjnAqgWw?t=8747" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:25:47</a> | Why would the Romans want Jesus' bloody garment in Matthew 27:35? | He said clothing was very valuable, Romans would not be ritually concerned about the blood, and the garments could be washed and reused or sold. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/F3BEjnAqgWw?t=9183" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:33:03</a> | Is Progressive Christianity really Christianity? | He answered no, arguing that it denies core doctrines and functions more like Christian-flavored moralism than historic Christianity. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/F3BEjnAqgWw?t=9306" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:35:06</a> | Did ancient Egyptians celebrate birthdays? | He said ordinary Egyptians do not seem to have celebrated birthdays in the way modern people do, though rulers could mark such occasions differently. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/F3BEjnAqgWw?t=9421" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:37:01</a> | Can you go over chronological methods for dating Pharaohs' reigns versus the Hittite sack of Babylon? | He discussed synchronisms, king lists, regnal years, treaties, wine dockets, and the uncertainty ranges that differ across Egyptian, Babylonian, and Hittite chronologies. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/F3BEjnAqgWw?t=10072" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:47:52</a> | Has your paper on the grain-price knowledge been published yet? | Not yet. He said he had heard from the editor and had to upload the correct paper. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/F3BEjnAqgWw?t=10606" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:56:46</a> | Does your chronology account for plus-or-minus dating ranges from sites and radiocarbon work? | Yes. He said the model incorporates asymmetrical error bars rather than pretending chronology can be calculated with false precision. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/F3BEjnAqgWw?t=11017" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:03:37</a> | Is there Christological development in the Gospels? | He said yes, but from a high-Christology view it means an unveiling of who Christ is, not Christ becoming divine over time. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/F3BEjnAqgWw?t=11125" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:05:25</a> | Have you heard of the Dead South, and if so, what are your thoughts? | He had not heard of the group and could not offer an opinion. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/F3BEjnAqgWw?t=11580" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:13:00</a> | Can you steelman Isaiah 53 not being about Christ? | He said he could not do that credibly because he finds the passage too strongly Messianic, though he acknowledged that some rabbis and others argue otherwise. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/F3BEjnAqgWw?t=11854" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:17:34</a> | Could Elizabeth have taken a Nazirite vow for John the Baptist? | He said she would not be taking the vow in the ordinary personal sense, but John could still have been dedicated as a Nazirite. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/F3BEjnAqgWw?t=12078" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:21:18</a> | Is God's abode above the waters or part of the expanse in Genesis 1? | He said the question presses the cosmological language too literally; Genesis depicts God's spirit over the waters during creation but does not locate God's spiritual abode inside the created expanse. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/F3BEjnAqgWw?t=12480" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:28:00</a> | Is it significant that the creation account does not explicitly mention the spiritual realm and spiritual beings the way other religions do? | He said Genesis uses analogical language and does address the spiritual realm, but not in the explicit mythic style some other texts use. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/F3BEjnAqgWw?t=13549" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:45:49</a> | How accurate is it to use lunisolar chronology and calendars? | He said the accuracy depends on the calendar's intercalation system; if it keeps the calendar aligned with the solar year, it can be reasonable. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/F3BEjnAqgWw?t=13660" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:47:40</a> | What is the theology behind criminals being buried in unconsecrated ground and Christians in blessed ground near a church? | He said that depends entirely on the denomination, because burial rules and theology vary by tradition. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/F3BEjnAqgWw?t=13845" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:50:45</a> | What Bible details surprise you when rereading? | He pointed to details such as Judah being the largest contingent in both censuses in Numbers, while noting such details become striking on rereading. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/F3BEjnAqgWw?t=14393" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:59:53</a> | Could "Ur of the Chaldees" be a redaction by scribes? | He said it could be an error or emendation, but he would be hesitant to call it a redaction because that implies an intentional content-changing alteration. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/F3BEjnAqgWw?t=14570" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:02:50</a> | What goal would a scribe have in changing Avaris to Rameses? | He said early Exodus date advocates might argue it was an updating of an unfamiliar place name, but he thinks that kind of redaction theory creates larger textual problems. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/F3BEjnAqgWw?t=14675" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:04:35</a> | For the early Exodus redaction theory, what would Avaris look like in typical Hebrew or Amorite? | He said Avaris and Rameses do not connect well phonetically or etymologically, and other route names create similar problems for the theory. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/F3BEjnAqgWw?t=15097" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:11:37</a> | If a self-identified Christian scholar refuses to affirm publicly that Christ rose from the dead, should they be considered Christian? | He answered no, because belief in the resurrection of Christ is central to Christian identity. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/F3BEjnAqgWw?t=15187" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:13:07</a> | How should Deuteronomy 22:28-29 be distinguished from Deuteronomy 22:25-26? | He said 22:25-26 describes a non-consensual field assault with a presumption of the woman's innocence, while 22:28-29 concerns a discovered consensual encounter. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/F3BEjnAqgWw?t=15960" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:26:00</a> | In Numbers 5:17, what is the holy water? | He explained it as water used in the priestly ordeal, combined with dust from the tabernacle floor in the ritual context. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/F3BEjnAqgWw?t=16122" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:28:42</a> | What are your thoughts on Jordan Peterson's <em>We Who Wrestle with God</em>? | He had not read it, so he declined to comment on the book's argument. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/F3BEjnAqgWw?t=16153" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:29:13</a> | What are your thoughts on Wesley Huff's debate with Billy Carson? | He thought the debate went badly for Carson, while noting that public apologetics also brings scrutiny and pressure on Huff. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/F3BEjnAqgWw?t=16346" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:32:26</a> | What are the ten toes in Daniel referring to? | He treated the passage as an eschatological prophecy question and handled it cautiously because such passages are interpretively difficult. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/F3BEjnAqgWw?t=17153" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:45:53</a> | How do you foresee AI affecting academic scholarship, archaeology, and biblical exegesis? | He called AI a tool, expected more plagiarism problems in scholarship, and thought archaeology would be less directly affected than language-processing work. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/F3BEjnAqgWw?t=17276" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:47:56</a> | What should be done when a scholar repeatedly gets the scholarship wrong on an issue? | He said the main thing is to stick to the evidence; persistent refusal to correct errors may warrant public critique. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/F3BEjnAqgWw?t=17442" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:50:42</a> | What are your thoughts on the Elephantine papyri? | He said they are an important source for Israelite culture in Egypt, especially for understanding an exiled Israelite community. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/F3BEjnAqgWw?t=17542" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:52:22</a> | How did Hellenistic Jews view pagan gods? | He said post-exilic Jews had learned from the exile and generally were not inclined to return to polytheism, even while engaging the surrounding world. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/F3BEjnAqgWw?t=17816" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:56:56</a> | Are Philistines and Phoenicians of the same stock, and how should they be distinguished? | He distinguished Philistines as Sea Peoples and Phoenicians as a Canaanite-related coastal population with different cultural development. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/F3BEjnAqgWw?t=18097" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">5:01:37</a> | How did David overcome stage fright or fear of public speaking as a teenager? | He said he joined Toastmasters and recommended that kind of practice for becoming comfortable speaking publicly. |
